target.title: fix(auto-reply): unify approval resolution with queue task state
target.body:
## Summary

AI-assisted PR.

- Problem: stale `/approve` IDs failed with a raw gateway error, and active-run follow-up queueing silently returned `undefined` even when a message was queued or rejected for backlog overflow.
- Why it matters: operators had no recovery hint for expired approvals, and users could not tell whether follow-up input was accepted, dropped, or blocked by queue pressure.
- What changed: mapped stale approval resolution errors to a recovery message, added structured follow-up enqueue results, and surfaced explicit queued/backlog-full acknowledgements from `runReplyAgent`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no runtime/task-queue prototype scripts were migrated into the repo, no approval policy semantics changed, and no new executor/runtime surface was added.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `/approve <id> ...` now tells the operator when the approval ID is no longer pending and asks for a fresh approval prompt instead of only surfacing `unknown approval id`.
- When a turn is already active and follow-up queueing is enabled, the reply layer now confirms that the message was queued and reports backlog position.
- If that backlog is full, the reply layer now returns an explicit error message instead of silently swallowing the follow-up.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local Node/Vitest
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): auto-reply command handling and follow-up queue runtime
- Relevant config (redacted): default local test config

### Steps

1. Trigger `/approve <stale-id> allow-once` after the backing approval request has expired or been resolved.
2. Send a non-heartbeat follow-up while another turn is already active and queue mode is `collect`.
3. Repeat with the follow-up backlog already at cap.

### Expected

- Stale approval IDs should return a recovery hint.
- Accepted follow-ups should report that they were queued.
- Queue overflow should produce an explicit error.

### Actual

- Verified by tests after this patch.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: ran targeted Vitest coverage for `/approve` fallback handling, detailed queue enqueue results, and active-run follow-up acknowledgements.
- Edge cases checked: duplicate queue entries, backlog-full queue rejection, stale approval ID recovery hint.
- What you did **not** verify: full end-to-end channel delivery against live integrations.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert commits `ddb56b8df7` and `b9427c73c4`.
- Files/config to restore: `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-approve.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/queue.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/enqueue.ts`, and related tests.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: follow-up messages no longer acknowledging queueing state, or `/approve` masking non-stale gateway failures.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk:
  - A stale-ID error matcher could accidentally catch unrelated gateway failures if upstream wording changes.
  - Mitigation: the matcher is narrowly scoped to known stale-ID phrases and preserves the original error text for all other failures.
- Risk:
  - Returning explicit follow-up acknowledgements could regress callers that assumed `undefined` on queued follow-ups.
  - Mitigation: added focused e2e coverage around active-run queue behavior and kept duplicate-drop behavior unchanged.
